title: US federal science spending correlates with suicides by hanging (r = 0.998)
description: Total US spending on science, space, and technology tracked suicides by hanging, strangulation, and suffocation at r = 0.998 from 1999–2009. The near-perfect fit — achieved with zero causal mechanism — is a favourite example in discussions about reproducibility, HARKing (Hypothesising After Results Known), and why effect size thresholds alone cannot substitute for theoretical justification.
